question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `NetworkSupply.releasedSupply()` arithmetic by supplying a timestamp that returns a `NetworkSupply` record where `unreleasedSupply > TOTAL_SUPPLY`, causing `releasedSupply()` to return a negative value (long underflow), which `convertToCurrencyFormat()` then formats as a negative supply figure, reorganizing the apparent supply history with invalid data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `q=CIRCULATING` with `timestamp=lte:0` to `getSupply()` to force `networkService.getSupply(bound)` to query at timestamp 0 (genesis), causing `convertToCurrencyFormat()` to format a zero or near-zero supply value as the current circulating supply, reorganizing the apparent supply history to show a false genesis state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=DESC` to `getNodes()` to reverse the node listing order, causing `linkFactory.create()` to generate pagination links based on the last node in descending order, reorganizing the apparent node history and causing clients to traverse nodes in reverse chronological order?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `file.id=eq:<non_existent_file_id>` to `getNodes()` to cause `networkService.getNetworkNodes(request)` to return an empty node list, making the API report no network nodes at a given point in time and reorganizing the apparent network topology history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `file.id=gt:0` to `getNodes()` to trigger the `IllegalArgumentException` ('Only equality operator is supported for file.id'), and use the error response to probe which file IDs are valid in the historical record, enabling targeted reorganization of node history queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=25` (the `MAX_LIMIT` boundary in `NetworkNodeRequest.getEffectiveLimit()`) combined with `order=DESC` to `getNodes()` to cause the pagination link to point to the last node in descending order, reorganizing the apparent node traversal history for clients following the `next` link?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=26` (above `MAX_LIMIT=25`) to `getNodes()` to test whether `getEffectiveLimit()` silently caps the value at 25 without returning an error, and use the discrepancy between the requested and returned page size to infer the total number of nodes in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple `node.id` range parameters to `getNodes()` (e.g., `node.id=gte:0&node.id=lte:999999`) to cause `networkService.getNetworkNodes(request)` to return all nodes in a single page, bypassing the intended pagination and reorganizing the apparent node history into a single flat snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `node.id=eq:0` to `getNodes()` to retrieve only the genesis node, and then use the returned node data (including its `consensusTimestamp`) to anchor a timestamp-based reorganization of the network history, identifying the exact genesis timestamp for further exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=DESC` to `getRegisteredNodes()` to reverse the registered node listing, causing `linkFactory.create()` with `REGISTERED_NODE_EXTRACTOR` to generate a `next` pagination link pointing to the last registered node in descending order, reorganizing the apparent registered node history for clients following pagination?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registerednode.id=gte:0&registerednode.id=lte:999999` to `getRegisteredNodes()` with `limit=100` (the `@Max(MAX_LIMIT)` boundary) to retrieve all registered nodes in a single page, bypassing pagination and reorganizing the apparent registered node history into a single flat snapshot?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `type=<invalid_value>` to `getRegisteredNodes()` to probe the `RegisteredNodeType` enum parsing, and use the error response to enumerate valid node types, enabling targeted reorganization of registered node history queries by type?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registerednode.id=eq:0&registerednode.id=eq:0` (duplicate EQ parameters, within `@Size(max=2)`) to `getRegisteredNodes()` to cause `Bound` to process duplicate operators, potentially causing `verifySingleOccurrence()` to throw an error that leaks internal state about the registered node history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=1` to `getRegisteredNodes()` and follow the `next` pagination link repeatedly to enumerate all registered nodes one by one, using the `registerednode.id` values in the links to reconstruct the complete registered node history in a reorganized order?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:<very_large_value>&timestamp=lte:<very_large_value+1>` to `getExchangeRate()` to query a far-future timestamp, causing `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` to return the most recent exchange rate record (as no future record exists), making the API report the current rate as a future historical rate and reorganizing the apparent exchange rate history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:1` (near-genesis timestamp) to `getExchangeRate()` to cause `fileService.getExchangeRate(bound)` to return the genesis exchange rate, making the API report a historical rate from the network's inception as if it were a valid current rate, reorganizing the apparent exchange rate history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getExchangeRate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:T&timestamp=lte:T` (same value for both bounds) to `getExchangeRate()`, causing `Bound.hasEqualBounds()` to return true and `adjustLowerRange()` to collapse the range to a single EQ point, and use this to precisely probe the exchange rate at any specific historical timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:<value>` where the value is exactly `feeScheduleExpirationTime * DomainUtils.NANOS_PER_SECOND` (the boundary in `getEffectiveFeeSchedule()`) to `getFees()`, causing a one-nanosecond difference to flip between current and next fee schedule, reorganizing the apparent fee history at the exact expiry boundary?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:<value>` where the value causes `getReferenceTimestampNanos()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE` (via `adjustUpperBound()` returning `Long.MAX_VALUE` when no upper bound is set), causing `getEffectiveFeeSchedule()` to always select the next fee schedule, reorganizing the apparent fee history to always show future fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gt:<value>` (without an upper bound) to `getFees()` to cause `Bound.adjustUpperBound()` to return `Long.MAX_VALUE`, which `getReferenceTimestampNanos()` then uses to fall back to the file's own `consensusTimestamp`, silently ignoring the user-supplied lower bound and reorganizing the apparent fee history reference point?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:0` to `getFees()` to cause `Bound.getAdjustedLowerRangeValue()` to return 0 (genesis), and combined with no upper bound, cause `getReferenceTimestampNanos()` to use the file's `consensusTimestamp` as reference, effectively ignoring the lower bound and returning the most recent fee schedule regardless of the requested range?
